items.briefing:
Phishing attacks are becoming more sophisticated and widespread, with AI now being used to craft convincing fake emails at scale — making it harder than ever to spot scams. This is particularly relevant for RTO staff who handle enrolments, payments, and student data, as these are attractive targets. Separately, Google has begun rolling out a feature on Android phones that can detect suspicious calls in real time, which may help staff identify phone-based scams.

If your organisation uses SolarWinds Serv-U file transfer software, ensure it is patched immediately, as attackers are actively exploiting a known flaw to crash servers.

**Tip:** Before clicking any link in an email, hover over it to preview the actual web address — if it looks unfamiliar or suspicious, don't click it.

featured.title: Google rolls out fake call detection on Android phones
featured.quote: Android users now get protection against spoofed calls impersonating trusted contacts.
